value_picks.name: EPAM Systems, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $692.9M / market cap $5.31B = 13.0% yield, well above the 7% pass line.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 7.21 vs implied PE 10.07 (target median 142.0 / fwd EPS 14.11) = 28.4% discount, just shy of the 30% pass threshold.

drawdown.detail: Down 54.2% from 52w high of $222.53; deep drawdown.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash positive: cash $1.04B vs debt $0.29B = +$0.75B. D/E 8.4, current ratio 2.67.

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout ratio 0). Counts as neutral / 0.5 per framework.

institutional.detail: Institutions held 118% (figure >100% implies securities-lending/short double-count, still >75%); rec_mean 1.89 (<2.5); 12 of 19 strong-buy/buy.

ai_exposure.detail: Two-sided: generative AI threatens the billable-hours, labor-arbitrage IT-services model, which is the fear driving the 54% drawdown; but EPAM also sells AI/data/cloud engineering services as a growth vector. Net neutral with the market currently pricing the threat.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Notable from the data: short interest 25.08% of float, up 34.77% MoM, days-to-cover 5.55 — a heavy and rising short base that creates squeeze potential if the AI-disruption thesis softens.

catalysts_general: Forward PE of 7.2 with PEG 0.51 — earnings growth 18.8% priced at deep value.
Trading ~28% below the $142 median analyst target.
Net cash balance sheet (+$0.75B) and 13% FCF yield provide downside support.
Short interest 25% and rising 35% MoM sets up a potential squeeze.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52-week low as AI-disruption fears on IT services persist.
Break below low on a demand air-pocket / recession hit to discretionary IT spend.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near the $89.25 52-week low (PE ~6.4) where the net-cash balance sheet and 13% FCF yield cap downside; buying the low rather than chasing the bounce off SMA20.

comparables.note: Larger Technology peer (input), also deeply discounted on forward earnings.
Direct IT-services peer Accenture trades at a materially higher forward multiple; EPAM's 7.2x is the cheapest in the cohort, reflecting AI-disruption fears.
Cognizant is the closest pure-play IT-services comp; referenced externally, not in input data.

value_picks.analyst_take: Deep value with a clean balance sheet: forward PE 7.2, PEG 0.51, 13% FCF yield and net cash, down 54% on AI-disruption fear. The 25% (and rising) short interest is both the key risk and a squeeze catalyst. A BUY for value investors who believe EPAM pivots into AI engineering rather than being disintermediated by it.

catalysts_general: Trailing PE 6.9; forward PE 9.5 reflects analyst expectation of lower gas-price-driven EPS (fwd EPS 9.71 vs trailing 13.44).
Trading ~30% below the $133 median target.

Revenue growth +41% with 34% operating margins; low beta 0.35.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Drift to near the $91 52-week low support on soft Henry Hub gas prices.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just under the $91 multi-year low support, where the 3.44% yield rises toward 3.6%+ and the FCF/valuation floor is firmest; price is already below SMA20 and SMA50.

value_picks.analyst_take: The cheapest, most defensive name here on a through-cycle basis: trailing PE 6.9, 3.4% well-covered dividend, 41% revenue growth, and the strongest analyst conviction in the cohort (rec_mean 1.58). The bear case is gas prices (forward EPS down vs trailing), but the AI-power demand tailwind and low beta make this a BUY for a gas-leveraged value sleeve.

WATCHLIST: wait for a retest of the $224 low or evidence the Firefly response is holding share before committing.
